bullets.text: Net premiums written grew 10% and earned premiums grew 8% for Q1 2005; Direct Personal Lines grew 15%, Agency grew 6%, and Commercial Auto grew 16%.
Net income of $413 million was strong but $47 million below Q1 2004's exceptional 83.2% combined ratio; all segments remain very profitable with management accepting the year-over-year decline.
Claims quality continued to rise; concierge-level service expansion accelerated with site-finding underway, targeting more than doubling the number of centers over two years.
Drive Insurance national TV commercials began airing; agents positioned as primary service providers for their customers, reducing long-term channel servicing costs for Progressive.
Internet-initiated quoting and sales identified as the most notable marketing trend; Progressive Direct brand focused on online sales ease and credible price comparison.
Special Lines products (motorcycle, boat, motor home) posted about 27% written premium growth in Q1; New Jersey market entry anticipated in the second half of 2005.
Quality of growth was encouraging with new customers and improved retention more than offsetting lower per-customer premiums in the soft market environment.
